Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Group A Protected Group All cases have LocalResident FALSE CommunityGroup None P001 Score 720 no adjustment Approved P002 Score 710 no adjustment Rejected P003 Score 740 no adjustment Approved Approval rate 2 3 66 7 Scores at decision 720 Approved 710 Rejected 740 Approved

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Interesting P002 with 710 was rejected while P001 with 720 approved So there is some threshold between 710 and 720 presumably Lets say threshold is around 715 or 720

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Group B Unprotected Group All cases have LocalResident TRUE U001 Score 720 CommunityGroup Highland Civic Darts Club plus 10 boost 730 Approved U002 Score 710 CommunityGroup None no adjustment 710 Rejected U003 Score 695 CommunityGroup Highland Civic Darts Club plus 10 boost 705 Approved Approval rate 2 3 66 7

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Wait but U003 with adjusted score 705 was approved while U002 with 710 was rejected And in Group A P002 with 710 was rejected So the decision threshold seems to be somewhere P001 720 approved P002 710 rejected U003 with 705 approved That is odd if the threshold is between 710 and 720 Unless the boost also directly influences the decision beyond the score or the threshold is lower for community group members

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Actually this is a key point U003 adjusted score is 705 which is below P002 710 rejected So U003 got approved despite having a lower score than a rejected case in Group A This suggests the Community Boost does not just adjust the score it may systematically tip decisions in favor of community group members or the threshold is applied differently

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Which log exhibits bias Group B Unprotected Group log exhibits bias because the ScoreAdjustment plus 10 Community Boost is applied based on CommunityGroup membership and LocalResident true Group A has ScoreAdjustment 0 for all cases no adjustments at all

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: How does bias manifest In Group B applicants who are local residents and members of the Highland Civic Darts Club receive a plus 10 score boost at PreliminaryScoring Those without community group membership U002 receive nothing In Group A no one receives any adjustment all are non local no community group The boost changes the effective score used at ManualReview and FinalDecision for example 695 to 705 and 720 to 730 Systematic difference The boost can push borderline applicants above the approval threshold U003 with a raw score of 695 lower than P002 710 rejected in Group A ends up approved So an applicant in Group B with a lower merit score gets approved while a higher scored applicant in Group A gets rejected This is disparate treatment or impact driven by LocalResident and CommunityGroup attributes

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Comparison of outcomes Group A approval seems merit based with threshold around 715 to 720 720 approved 710 rejected 740 approved Group B U001 730 approved consistent U002 710 rejected consistent U003 705 approved inconsistent with Group A implicit threshold Without the boost U003 695 would presumably be rejected The boost creates a double standard two applicants with similar profiles receive different outcomes based on community membership

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Fairness metrics Approval rates are equal 2 3 each so demographic parity at group level is satisfied superficially But the bias is within Group B members of the community group have 100 percent approval U001 U003 non member U002 rejected Also comparing similar raw scores across groups 710 in Group A rejected but 695 in Group B with boost approved This is individual fairness violation similar individuals treated differently Counterfactual fairness If U003 had not been in the community group score 695 likely rejected The causal influence of CommunityGroup on decision is direct

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The role of LocalResident The boost appears conditional on LocalResident true and CommunityGroup membership Group A LocalResident false never gets boosts So LocalResident acts as a gate only local residents can receive community boost The CommunityGroup attribute then determines who within Group B gets the boost

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Where in the process The bias enters at PreliminaryScoring where the plus 10 adjustment is applied by the Scoring Engine and propagates through ManualReview underwriter sees 730 Adjusted and 705 Adjusted and FinalDecision The ManualReview step does not correct it the underwriter sees the adjusted score which may anchor their view

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Systematic differences in final decisions The boost effectively lowers the approval threshold for community group members

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Actually let me check if the threshold were 705 then P002 710 would be approved in Group A But P002 was rejected with 710 So threshold must be more than 710 for Group A And U003 approved at 705 adjusted So either the threshold for Group B members is lower or the threshold is around 715 and U003 705 should have been rejected but was approved anyway meaning the decision for U003 is inconsistent with its own adjusted score Either way there is an inconsistency U003 approved at 705 while P002 rejected at 710 and U002 rejected at 710

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This is evidence that the Community Boost affects the decision beyond the nominal score that is disparate treatment The community membership is effectively a decision factor

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Group A P001 720 Approved P002 710 Rejected P003 740 Approved Implicit threshold between 711 and 720 Group B U001 720 plus 10 equals 730 Approved U002 710 plus 0 equals 710 Rejected U003 695 plus 10 equals 705 Approved If threshold were uniform say 715 or 720 U003 at 705 should be rejected So the boost true effect appears to be more than plus 10 in decision terms or the decision rule directly incorporates community membership

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - PE
Text: Without boost U003 raw 695 lower than rejected cases would be rejected With boost approved So the boost flipped a decision That is concrete evidence of bias impact

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Also note selection of who gets the boost LocalResident true and CommunityGroup not None U002 is local but no group no boost So the gating attribute is CommunityGroup membership within locals In Group A nobody is local so no boost possible the eligibility rule itself is tied to LocalResident

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: The answer should note Group B exhibits bias the treatment encoded in Group B process Manifestation conditional score boost tied to local residency community club membership applied at scoring carried through manual review and final decision it creates an uneven playing field results in approval of lower scoring applicants over higher scoring ones 705 adjusted approved versus 710 rejected revealing inconsistent thresholds Also note within Group B discrimination U002 local resident but no club membership gets no boost and is rejected club members both approved Group level approval parity 2 3 versus 2 3 masks the underlying bias

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: Depending on interpretation the boost could be a deliberate affirmative action style adjustment But the question asks which log exhibits bias Group B log because it contains the differential ScoreAdjustment tied to sensitive attributes
